commit.message:
Assumed median scores for non-voting players (#15)

When voting closes with some players not having voted on certain songs,
scores are imbalanced. This adds median-based assumed scores for missing
votes so every song is scored by every player.

### Scoring logic

- `computeMedianScore(scores)` — median of actual votes, `Math.floor`
for even-length arrays
- `scoreToEmoji(score)` — rounds down to the largest emoji score ≤ the
computed median
- Both live in `src/app/shared/scoring.ts` as pure functions with 13
unit tests

```ts
computeMedianScore([5, 3])  // → 4
scoreToEmoji(4)              // → "😁" (score 3, the largest ≤ 4)
```

### Data layer (`data.ts`)

- `SongResult.voteBreakdown` entries gain an `assumed: boolean` field
- `getResultsByScore` now fetches voters, identifies who didn't vote per
song, and fills in assumed votes using the median — these count toward
`totalScore`
- New `getAssumedVotesForVoter(gameId, voterName)` returns assumed
ratings for the vote page

### Vote page

- When game is closed, unrated songs show the assumed emoji with a
dashed border, 50% opacity, and an "(assumed)" label
- No visual change for songs the voter actually rated

**Bob's vote page — mix of actual votes (Moldova, Sweden) and assumed
votes (Croatia onward):**

![Bob's vote page with mixed actual and
assumed](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d620a1ea-ca6c-452b-ad1d-806fae63adb2)

### Results reveal

- Actual and assumed votes are separated in the breakdown
- Assumed votes render under an "Assumed (didn't vote):" sub-heading
with dashed border styling
- Section is hidden when all players voted

**Results reveal — Sweden scored by Alice & Bob, with Charlie's assumed
median vote:**

![Results reveal with assumed
votes](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/efdd26a4-373e-4ae7-95d0-cf823c3e3856)

### Infrastructure

- Added `vitest` (separate `vitest.config.ts` to avoid Cloudflare Vite
plugin conflict) with `pnpm run test:unit`

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: valscion <482561+valscion@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: keep radio session alive on queue exhaustion

- Send paused=False (not True) in completion signal so the YM app
  radio engine stays active and pushes more tracks
- Wait 30s in the stream loop for new tracks instead of stopping
  immediately — gives the controller time to populate the queue
- Only set _stream_stop_event after timeout, not on exhaustion

The previous paused=True caused the YM app to end the radio session,
preventing new tracks from being pushed. With paused=False, the device
appears 'still active' and the radio engine keeps working.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: skip add_labels in build-test on workflow_dispatch (#1914)

The build-test workflow fails on workflow_dispatch because the agent
calls add_labels when there's no PR context. The safe_outputs handler
for add_labels errors with 'No issue/PR number available' unlike
add_comment which gracefully skips.

Update the prompt to instruct the agent to only add labels when
triggered by a pull_request event.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Repo Assist] perf: cache MethodInfo for asyncCast/taskCast to avoid repeated reflection (#374)

* perf: cache MethodInfo for asyncCast/taskCast to avoid repeated reflection

GetMethod and MakeGenericMethod are called on every API response
deserialization. The GetMethod result is constant (same method every
time), and MakeGenericMethod produces the same MethodInfo for a given
return type. Cache both at module level to eliminate the per-call
overhead.

- asyncCastMethod / taskCastMethod: computed once at module init
- asyncCastCache / taskCastCache: ConcurrentDictionary<Type, MethodInfo>
  to memoize MakeGenericMethod per return type

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

* refactor: use GetMethods+filter for cast method resolution, extract helper

commit.message:
Add Apple Silicon (MPS) support for fine-tuning

- Detect CUDA / MPS / CPU with _get_device()
- Skip 4-bit quantization on MPS (bitsandbytes CUDA-only)
- Load model in float16 directly on MPS
- Use bf16=True training on MPS, fp16=True on CUDA
- Remove CUDA-only hard error in main()

128GB Apple Silicon Mac can fine-tune Llama 3 8B in float16 (~16GB).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove .value from shallowRef in template (auto-unwrapped by Vue)

shallowRef is a top-level ref in <script setup>, so Vue auto-unwraps it
in the template. Writing imageOptions.value in the template accesses a
non-existent property on the inner object → undefined → 500 error.

- Template: imageOptions.value.cropEnabled → imageOptions.cropEnabled
- Template: imageOptions.value.crop → imageOptions.crop
- Template: :options="imageOptions.value" → :options="imageOptions"
- Script functions (onExport, onGenerateGif) retain .value correctly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement SQLite storage backend (#5)

* Implement SQLite backend for celer-mem

Add SQLiteBackend class satisfying the StorageBackend concept, with:
- WAL journal mode for better concurrency
- Busy timeout configuration
- RAII lifecycle management
- Full key-value operations: get, put, del, prefix_scan, batch, compact, foreach_scan
- One SQLite db file per table (same isolation model as RocksDB backend)
- 13 dedicated tests covering all operations, persistence, 10K records, concurrency

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Solesius/celer-mem/sessions/f02ff92c-c356-4d2f-a0d0-bba284d27e7e

Co-authored-by: Solesius <38218017+Solesius@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: SQLite backend uses real SQL tables per scope instead of separate db files

Each logical table is now a real SQL table inside a shared scope-level
.db file (e.g. project.db contains "tasks" and "notes" tables), rather
than creating separate .db files per table with a generic "kv" table.

This properly mirrors how RocksDB column families partition a single DB:
- One sqlite3 connection per scope (shared via shared_ptr)
- Each logical table → CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS "<table>" (...)
- batch() uses SAVEPOINT for safe nesting on shared connections
- Factory caches scope connections in an internal map
- Makefile auto-detects sqlite3 like it does rocksdb

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Solesius/celer-mem/sessions/14e50cde-4434-4ecc-8315-de02942e1a80

Co-authored-by: Solesius <38218017+Solesius@users.noreply.github.com>

* security: harden SQLite backend against SQL injection via table/scope names

Add validate_ident() that enforces a strict [a-zA-Z0-9_] allowlist for
all table and scope names before they reach any SQL. Also harden
quote_ident() to strip NUL bytes as defense-in-depth. Add test covering
injection attempts (semicolons, NUL bytes, quotes, path traversal).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Solesius/celer-mem/sessions/0b7b3406-7f0e-4272-a486-1f8eeb249b2a

Co-authored-by: Solesius <38218017+Solesius@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Solesius <38218017+Solesius@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement freeform CSG subtract/merge with explicit geometry, crash fix, and cutter visual differentiation

- Add BrushGeometry with explicit faces/vertices for boolean result brushes
- Implement BSP-based BrushBooleanKernel for subtract and merge operations
- Subtract: selected cutter carves all intersecting brushes, cutter consumed
- Merge: selected brushes combined into single result brush
- Tag cutter-sourced faces (IsCutterFace) for visual differentiation
- Render cutter inner-wall wireframes at 30% alpha in all viewports
- Fix GL thread-safety crash: move buffer pruning into renderer Render() methods
- Add BrushBounds and hit-testing support for explicit geometry brushes
- Persist explicit geometry in map format with backward compatibility
- Add compact toolbar layout with grouped primitive and boolean actions
- Add dark toolbar button template to prevent white styling artifacts
- 82 tests passing (28 core, 12 formats, 28 app, 14 rendering)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: cover --global branches in list and install commands

Add two integration tests to cli_tests.rs that exercise the previously
uncovered True branches at main.rs:979 (install --global) and main.rs:997
(list --global):

- list_global_no_installed_plugins: runs `aipm list --global` with an
  isolated HOME directory (no installed.json), verifying the empty-registry
  path prints 'No globally installed plugins.'
- install_global_local_plugin: runs `aipm install --global local:./my-plugin`
  with an isolated HOME, verifying success output and that installed.json
  is written with the spec.

Branch coverage: 93.50% → 93.74% (TOTAL), main.rs: 65.31% → 70.41%.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: Remotion tutorial project with 18 compositions

- 10 tutorial lessons: HelloRemotion, TimingPlayground, SequencingDemo, TypewriterEffect, WordHighlight, MediaShowcase, AnimatedBarChart, PieChartReveal, SceneTransitions, KitchenSink
- 4 CSS mastery compositions: TransformShowcase, ClipPathReveal, FilterEffects, SVGDrawing
- Scene sync framework with dynamic hold points (SceneSyncDemo)
- FullTutorialVideo: all 10 lessons as scenes
- Arrow timeline template with transitionFrames (GettingStarted, ToolkitWorkflow)
- Reusable libs: TutorialBackground, DebugPanel, ScenePlayer, ArrowTimeline
- createSceneComposition() + wrapAsScene() factory
- Interactive HTML tutorial (index.html)
- npm package: @skyhawk14/remotion-scene-toolkit@0.1.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: remotion-tutor
repository.description: Learn Remotion through 18 interactive compositions — scene framework, hold points, arrow timelines, CSS mastery, data viz, and more

commit.message:
Fix flaky UDP server failure integration test (#1247)

Use XUnitStaticHelpers.Retry with the xUnit cancellation token for the UDP query in Udp_ServerFailure_WhenNoHandlerConfigured so transient listener startup timing does not fail the test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add mobile support

- Full-screen scaling on mobile (100vw/dvh)
- Landscape orientation hint overlay for portrait mode
- Always-visible hotspot labels on touch devices (no hover needed)
- Disable pinch-zoom and touch-action for cleaner gameplay
- PWA-capable meta tags

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove PMApi from SDK

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove remote provider API usage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: providers (Bedrock, vision, model filter, tool choice, response format), storage (fork, memory bank, tracer), sandbox (bash validation, ignore filter, container)

- Bedrock adapter with AWS SigV4 signing
- Vision support (Image content blocks) for Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini
- ModelFilter with allow/deny lists
- ToolChoice control mapped per provider
- ResponseFormat (JSON mode) mapped per provider
- Session fork with message copying
- Structured memory bank helpers
- Durable session tracer with schema v4 migration
- BashValidator with safe/caution/dangerous classification
- .caduceusignore integration via IgnoreFilter
- ContainerSandbox with Docker volume mounting
- 18 new tests across all crates

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Scribe: Document Aech CPM centralization outcome

- Orchestration log (2026-04-11T17-13-00-aech.md): Task SUCCESS
- Session log (2026-04-11T17-13-00-build-config.md): Build config complete
- Decisions merged: Deduped aech-cpm-centralized and copilot-directive-issue-tracking
- Aech history updated: CPM requires pinned versions, not wildcards

Outcomes:
  - Solution builds clean (commit 4a9d696)
  - All 14 .csproj files stripped; properties inherited from repo root
  - Central package management (CPM) enabled with pinned versions
  - No floating wildcards supported; all versions from NuGet cache

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix legacy QRZ dotenv compatibility

Retry .env loading with a narrow compatibility rewrite for legacy unquoted LOGRIPPER_QRZ_USER_AGENT values and add regression coverage that preserves later QRZ settings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(dna): add FederationSchemaController, fix QueryPolicy 400, add i18n keys

- Create FederationSchemaController for /federation/schemas API (fixes 404)
- Fix QueryPolicy page to load data sources instead of catalogs (fixes 400)
- Add 78 missing i18n keys for DataAsset/DataQuality/DataLineage entities
  across zh-CN, en-US, ja-JP locales (fixes raw key display in tables)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
